name: pm-scripts
description: Validation and reporting scripts for the PM folder. Run `node 93-Scripts/install.js` (or wire `pm:install`) ONCE to auto-add every pm:* script to the project's root package.json, write the path map, pin the folder layout, and register the Claude Code hooks (guarded); then `pm:doctor` verifies the wiring.

scripts.pm:all: node validate-frontmatter.js && node generate-monitor.js && node generate-dashboard.js

note: These scripts are dependency-free (Node stdlib only). FASTEST PATH: run `node _00-Project-Management/93-Scripts/install.js` once — it merges every pm:* script into the project's root package.json (idempotent) and pins the folder layout (full | flattened | auto-detected) in .claude-pm-config.json. Then `npm run pm:doctor` reports wiring health and exits non-zero if core scripts (pm:lint/pm:dash) are missing. The kit resolves PM sub-folder names through 93-Scripts/lib/pm-paths.js, so a project may use the canonical numbering (30-Epics, 32-Stories, …) OR a flattened layout (01-EPIC, 03-Stories, …) OR a custom `paths` map in .claude-pm-config.json. Manual wiring (if you prefer): "pm:lint": "node _00-Project-Management/93-Scripts/validate-frontmatter.js", "pm:dash": "node _00-Project-Management/93-Scripts/generate-dashboard.js", "pm:monitor": "node _00-Project-Management/93-Scripts/generate-monitor.js", "pm:map": "node _00-Project-Management/93-Scripts/generate-codebase-map.js", "pm:board": "node _00-Project-Management/93-Scripts/generate-backlog-board.js", "pm:doctor": "node _00-Project-Management/93-Scripts/doctor.js", "pm:install": "node _00-Project-Management/93-Scripts/install.js", "pm:all": "npm run pm:lint && npm run pm:monitor && npm run pm:dash", "pm:claude-scaffold": "node _00-Project-Management/93-Scripts/claude-scaffold.js", "pm:claude-audit": "node _00-Project-Management/93-Scripts/claude-audit.js", "pm:claude-hooks": "node _00-Project-Management/93-Scripts/install-hooks.js". pm:monitor regenerates MONITOR.md's count rollups between managed markers from live frontmatter — run it (or pm:all) after creating/deleting artefacts. Optionally add a Husky pre-commit hook to run pm:all on every commit.
